# Tyrekick

> Feedback loop for AI-built web prototypes. A ~12 KB (gzipped) dependency-free
> script adds a "Give feedback" button; reviewers pin comments on the live page
> (no accounts); structured JSON (element + visible text + heading + route + page
> errors) POSTs to a webhook the owner controls (Discord or their own Cloudflare
> Worker); coding agents pull feedback back over MCP (list_feedback /
> get_feedback / triage_feedback / resolve_feedback / feedback_stats) and fix
> what was flagged.

Install for a project: `npx tyrekick init --webhook <url> --yes` (injects the script
tag, sends a test comment, prints the MCP command). Details in AGENTS.md.

## Docs

- [AGENTS.md](AGENTS.md): exact install + feedback-loop steps for coding agents (incl. review modes and passive/active operating patterns)
- [README.md](README.md): human overview, config table, payload schema v2
- [docs/README.md](docs/README.md): full documentation index
- [docs/getting-started.md](docs/getting-started.md): install paths + destination choice by hosting risk
- [docs/configuration.md](docs/configuration.md): every config field / data-* attribute with defaults
- [docs/reviewing.md](docs/reviewing.md): reviewer-facing behaviour (pins, threads, drawer, keyboard/touch)
- [docs/destinations.md](docs/destinations.md): Discord vs Cloudflare Worker, REST surface, protecting public deploys
- [docs/agent-loop.md](docs/agent-loop.md): MCP setup, five tools, review modes, operating patterns, resolution-note conventions
- [docs/payload.md](docs/payload.md): schema v2 field-by-field reference
- [docs/troubleshooting.md](docs/troubleshooting.md): FAQ, storage keys, privacy guarantees, SPA notes
- [destinations/discord/README.md](destinations/discord/README.md): zero-code Discord destination
- [destinations/cloudflare/README.md](destinations/cloudflare/README.md): TypeScript worker destination + REST API (GET/PATCH /feedback, Bearer TYREKICK_TOKEN)
- [mcp/README.md](mcp/README.md): MCP server (npx tyrekick-mcp), tools, claude mcp add command

## Key facts

- One-command install: npx tyrekick init --webhook <url> --yes (non-interactive; auto-detects the HTML entry file)
- Script tag: <script src=".../dist/tyrekick.js" data-webhook="..." data-app-version="..."></script> (auto-init)
- ESM: npm install tyrekick; import { init } from "tyrekick"
- Payload schema: 2. Never capture input values. Reviewers never log in.
- MCP: npx tyrekick-mcp with env TYREKICK_URL + TYREKICK_TOKEN
